commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor proof plus secops review or a branch repair that preserves audit coverage for config-owned env secrets before merge.; Needs attention: The diff can create a security-audit false negative for secrets stored through config-authored env vars.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve findings for config-owned env refs — src/security/audit-extra.sync.ts:606-609 (sha=2db5e7b02f910f9c83f6d3a7c4959f653d7ea474)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes security-audit stored-secret checks to consult `ConfigFileSnapshot.parsed` values before suppressing gateway password and hooks token findings, with regression tests for env refs and plaintext literals.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +117. Total +155 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows the audit passes only resolved `cfg` into `collectSecretsInConfigFindings`, so authored `${VAR}` strings are gone before `looksLikeEnvRef` when the env var resolves.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof plus secops review or a branch repair that preserves audit coverage for config-owned env secrets before merge.

target.title: fix(security): avoid false-positive secret findings when config uses env references
target.body:
<!--
Closes #62438
-->

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `openclaw security audit` reported `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` (warn) and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` (info) for values that correctly use the documented `${ENV_VAR}` substitution syntax.

## Why This Change Was Made

The secrets-in-config collector was checking the resolved runtime config, where `${...}` placeholders had already been substituted by the config loader. `ConfigFileSnapshot.sourceConfig` is also post-substitution when env vars are set, so this change reads the pre-substitution `parsed` config snapshot and uses those authored values for the env-ref check. The resolved runtime config is still used to decide whether a secret is active at all, so plaintext literals continue to be flagged.

## User Impact

Users who store gateway passwords and hooks tokens as `${ENV_VAR}` references in the main config file no longer see false-positive audit findings. Plaintext literals in config are still flagged.

## Evidence

- Added regression tests in `src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` covering both env-ref suppression and continued plaintext flagging, using an explicit `configSnapshot` with pre-substitution `parsed` values.
- Targeted test runs: `pnpm test src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` and `pnpm test src/cli/security-cli.test.ts` pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` pass.
- Isolated script proof:
  - Env-ref case (`${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}`, `${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}`): `hits: []`
  - Literal plaintext case: both `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` are emitted.
- Real CLI proof (built from this branch, `pnpm build`, Node v22.22.0):

  Env-ref config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/envref node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 1 },
    \"hits\": []
  }
  ```

  Plaintext config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"literal-password\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"literal-token\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/plaintext node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 2 },
    \"hits\": [
      {
        \"checkId\": \"config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config\",
        \"severity\": \"warn\",
        \"title\": \"Gateway password is stored in config\",
        \"detail\": \"gateway.auth.password is set in the config file; prefer environment variables for secrets when possible.\",
        \"remediation\": \"Prefer OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD (env) and remove gateway.auth.password from disk.\"
      },
      {
        \"checkId\": \"config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config\",
        \"severity\": \"info\",
        \"title\": \"Hooks token is stored in config\",
        \"detail\": \"hooks.token is set in the config file; keep config perms tight and treat it like an API secret.\"
      }
    ]
  }
  ```
target.author: xydigit-sj

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
